content:
# Dhaiqa Formation

**Summary**: An Ediacaran sedimentary formation in Saudi Arabia characterized by carbonates and siliciclastics.

---

## How It Appears in the Thesis Collection

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] studied the Dhaiqa Formation to determine its depositional environment.

## Depositional Environment

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] mixed clastic-carbonate system, potentially lacustrine or marginal marine

## Reservoir Properties



## Diagenetic History

- [[2024-malik|Malik, 2024]] chertification and dolomitization

## Key Findings Across Sources

- The Dhaiqa Formation is predominantly carbonate interbedded with sandstone horizons, showing a transition from clastic-rich to clastic-poor environments. ([[2024-malik|Malik, 2024, p. 29]])
